chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: Functions are one of the fundamental building blocks in JavaScript.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: A function is a block of code designed to perform a particular task.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: You define a function using the function keyword followed by a name and parentheses.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: Parameters are listed inside the parentheses in the function definition.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: Arguments are the values passed to the function when it is invoked.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: The return statement stops the execution of a function and returns a value.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: A function without a return statement returns undefined.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: Function expressions assign a function to a variable.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: Arrow functions are a shorter syntax for writing function expressions.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: Arrow functions use the fat arrow syntax: const add equals parentheses arrow.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: If the function has only one expression, you can omit the curly braces and return keyword.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: Arrow functions do not have their own this keyword, which is important for objects.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: Default parameters allow you to set default values for function parameters.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: Rest parameters allow a function to accept an indefinite number of arguments as an array.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: Callback functions are functions passed as arguments to other functions.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: Callbacks are heavily used in JavaScript for asynchronous operations.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: Higher-order functions are functions that take other functions as arguments or return functions.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: Map, filter, and reduce are examples of higher-order functions.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: IIFE stands for Immediately Invoked Function Expression. It runs as soon as it is defined.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: Closures are functions that have access to variables from their outer scope even after the outer function has returned.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: Understanding closures is very important for advanced JavaScript programming.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: Functions are first-class citizens in JavaScript, meaning they can be treated like any other value.

chunks.title: JavaScript Functions & Arrow Functions
chunks.text: Practice writing functions as they are the backbone of any JavaScript application.

text: Functions are one of the fundamental building blocks in JavaScript. A function is a block of code designed to perform a particular task. You define a function using the function keyword followed by a name and parentheses. Parameters are listed inside the parentheses in the function definition. Arguments are the values passed to the function when it is invoked. The return statement stops the execution of a function and returns a value. A function without a return statement returns undefined. Function expressions assign a function to a variable. Arrow functions are a shorter syntax for writing function expressions. Arrow functions use the fat arrow syntax: const add equals parentheses arrow. If the function has only one expression, you can omit the curly braces and return keyword. Arrow functions do not have their own this keyword, which is important for objects. Default parameters allow you to set default values for function parameters. Rest parameters allow a function to accept an indefinite number of arguments as an array. Callback functions are functions passed as arguments to other functions. Callbacks are heavily used in JavaScript for asynchronous operations. Higher-order functions are functions that take other functions as arguments or return functions. Map, filter, and reduce are examples of higher-order functions. IIFE stands for Immediately Invoked Function Expression. It runs as soon as it is defined. Closures are functions that have access to variables from their outer scope even after the outer function has returned. Understanding closures is very important for advanced JavaScript programming. Functions are first-class citizens in JavaScript, meaning they can be treated like any other value. Practice writing functions as they are the backbone of any JavaScript application.
